item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I think the concept is alright, there's a couple of glaring issues I see with this one.

For one, there's tone issues in a lot of places. Several terms are used which are more descriptive than clinical, and there's a few sentences where imagery is more dominant.

Specimens are able to camouflage themselves by taking on the appearance of their environment. When cloaked, the light around a specimen appears to bend, and is the only indication of their presence.

This is relatively vague, and also non-clinical especially with the way the effect is not described in any scientific way. There's a lot of assumptions made with this section.

The containment procedures are also rather vague, with no real specifications for how the environment is to be set up.

Secondly, the last addenda is…troubling. The idea of the foundation declaring severe weather patterns worsening is due to an SCP and that climate change is not actually the cause, while…thematic to "the Foundation does what's necessary", irks me on a pretty deep level.

There's solid science behind severe weather and climate change being related, and this sort of throws it out the window somehow.

I'm sorry but at this point it's a downvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Aves" should not be italic. Genus and species are italic, but higher-order taxa are not. Also, it is customary (but not required), to abbreviate the genus to a single letter when a name is used the 2nd and later times. For example "Archilochus colubris is blah blah blah, and also A. colubris blah blah."
Source ICZN App B-6,11.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
we now have invisibirb and shapeshifter birb. technically we have phone bird and loud bird and translator birb and eldritch birb and impostor birb.

we must make a Mr. misters collection but birb.

Is birbs
+1000000000000

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A line by line
enil yb enil A

All currently contained instances

I think this would read better as "All instances of… currently in containment."

in containment chambers large enough for comfortable flight and exercise

Birds don't exactly exercise. I think "movement" might be a more appropriate word here.

due to the sheer number of currently contained specimens

Consider replacing "sheer" with something like "large", it just reads more clinically.

with two (2) "spray-mist" humidifiers

A. I generally advise against "number (#)" syntax. It's unnecessary and just sort of clutters the sentence.
B. I think the phrase "spray-mist" is unnecessary.

running at all times in each of their respective chambers.

This "their" technically refers to the containment chambers, so this sentence reads "in each of the containment chambers' containment chambers".

These species can differ greatly in appearance

This feels redundant. We know that different species of birds look different.

Specimens of the SCP

In-universe, objects are typically not referred to as "SCP"s but as "objects", "items", "entities", etc.

camouflage themselves by taking on the environment

What does this mean?

condensed water vapor (clouds)

I suggest cutting the "(clouds)", to make this read a little more clinically.

to have preferences in regard to cloud types: some prefer cirrus, some prefer cumulus

I suggest just ending this sentence after "types".

SCP-2285-7 infallibly prefer

"infallibly" is not a very scientific term.

what their biological lungs

As opposed to… their mechanical lungs? Their psychological lungs?

In addition, determining the difference between a regular Anodorhynchus glaucus and SCP-2285-3 requires at least seven (7) days of observation

Why?

As such, the Foundation must avoid attention from the IUCN

The IUCN is a functionally powerless entity that a vast and powerful entity like the Foundation would likely have zero problem sidestepping.

Despite being the largest bird, SCP-2285-6's preferred cloud is cirrus.

There really hasn't been any evidence put forward linking bird size and cloud-diet, so I think this bit is unnecessary.

SCP-2285-7's preferred clouds are those found in a supercell

So does this eagle also somehow have the ability to fly normally in incredibly intense wind?

As supercell clouds contain amounts of electricity

"amount of electricity" is not really scientific phrasing.

Whether this is due to the lack of SCP-2285 in natural environments is unknown

I understand that these are anomalous birds, but expecting me to believe that only 470 birds can control the entire world's weather patters is absurd.


Neutral vote from me. The idea is neat, but I don't think it's really developed enough, and there are a fair number of technical/stylistic issues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason, this reminded me of Cloud-Cuckoo-Land from "The Birds" of Aristophanes. In a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2285-4 exhibit no behavioral differences from their corresponding species.

I feel very, very bad for the poor researcher who enters SCP-2285-4's containment.

Edit: for those of you who haven't been attacked by a six-foot, territorial heron, I don't recommend the experience.
